evals.query: Set up a Kora Cassandra repository for a User entity (UUID id, name, email, createdAt) with findById, findAll, insert and deleteById.
expected_behavior: Adds the ru.tinkoff.kora:database-cassandra dependency and the kora-parent BOM, with the Java annotation-processors annotation processor (or ksp symbol-processors for Kotlin)
Adds CassandraDatabaseModule to the @KoraApp interface
Declares an interface annotated with @Repository that extends CassandraRepository
Annotates the DAO record with @EntityCassandra and uses @Column on each component
Writes CQL in @Query with named parameters like :id and returns @Nullable for the single-row lookup
Does not use JPA/Data-repository annotations, field injection, or invent an @Id requirement (Cassandra @Id is optional)

evals.query: Configure the Cassandra connection in HOCON: contact points, datacenter, keyspace, credentials from environment variables, and a 5 second request timeout.
expected_behavior: Places contactPoints, dc, and sessionKeyspace under cassandra.basic
Places timeout under cassandra.basic.request
Puts login and password under cassandra.auth and substitutes environment variables with ${CASSANDRA_USER} style references
Does not invent keys such as healthCheck, retryPolicy, loadBalancing.policy, or basic.connection

evals.query: Model a Cassandra entity with a nested Address user-defined type (street, city, zipCode) plus a list of addresses.
expected_behavior: Annotates the nested type record with @UDT
Annotates the owning DAO record with @EntityCassandra
Maps the collection as List<AddressUDT> and notes that CQL needs FROZEN for UDT collections
Does not use JPA @Embeddable / @Embedded for the Cassandra UDT

evals.query: Add a method that inserts many users in one batch.
expected_behavior: Marks the list parameter with @Batch (ru.tinkoff.kora.database.common.annotation.Batch)
Uses a single-row INSERT @Query bound to the entity fields, e.g. :entity.id
Returns void or another supported batch return type rather than a per-row result
Does not hand-build a BatchStatement or use an external ORM template

evals.query: I want non-blocking Cassandra access. Show CompletionStage and Project Reactor repository methods.
expected_behavior: Provides methods returning CompletableFuture<T> or CompletionStage<T>
Provides Mono<T> and Flux<T> methods and notes io.projectreactor:reactor-core is required for them
Keeps @Query CQL identical to the synchronous version, only changing the return type
Does not block a future with .get() inside the repository

evals.query: I need an analytics query to run with ONE consistency and a 30s timeout while the rest of the repository stays on QUORUM.
expected_behavior: Defines a profile under cassandra.profiles overriding basic.request.consistency and basic.request.timeout
Sets the default basic.request.consistency for the rest of the queries
Annotates the specific @Query method with @CassandraProfile("analytics")
Notes that @CassandraProfile is @Target(METHOD) and cannot be placed on the repository interface
Does not use a JDBC-style @Transactional annotation or invent readConsistency/writeConsistency keys

evals.query: Map a CQL int column to a Java enum on both read and write in a Cassandra repository.
expected_behavior: Implements CassandraRowColumnMapper for the read side (apply(GettableByName, int))
Implements CassandraParameterColumnMapper for the write side (apply(SettableByName, int, value))
Wires both mappers with @Mapping on the record component or query parameter
Does not rely on runtime reflection-based mapping

evals.query: Add a conditional insert that only writes when the row does not already exist (lightweight transaction).
expected_behavior: Writes CQL with IF NOT EXISTS in the @Query
Returns boolean to read the [applied] flag, or maps the conflicting row via @Nullable
Mentions that LWT uses serial consistency configured via basic.request.serialConsistency
Does not claim Cassandra supports JDBC-style multi-statement transactions

evals.query: Write a Kotlin Cassandra repository using coroutines and Flow.
expected_behavior: Declares an interface annotated with @Repository that extends CassandraRepository
Uses suspend functions and a fun returning Flow<T> for streaming
Notes that the build uses ksp "ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors" and requires kotlinx-coroutines-core
Uses @field:Column on data class properties
Does not use foreign-framework persistence annotations or field injection
